reason: Caterpillars feed in foliage and rest on bark — not default flower-visitor pollinators.
Generalist leaf-feeding caterpillars are anchored to foliage not flowers.
Specialist leaf beetle sits near-motionless on Crataegus foliage rather than visiting flowers.
Semi-aquatic mammals that patrol canal banks and swim through the water body.
Sap-feeding planthopper clings motionless to bark rather than visiting flowers.
Defoliating caterpillar feeds in canopy and rests on bark — not a flower-visitor.
Hawkmoths hover at blooms (flower-bobber) then make fast transit flights (sky-looper).
Specialist leafhopper feeds motionlessly on Gleditsia foliage undersides.
Communal caterpillar feeds in canopy and rests in silk mats on bark.
Lackey moth caterpillars feed in foliage and lay egg rings on bark — not flower-visitors.
Arboreal mustelid leaps through canopy by day and hunts on ground at night.
Bruchid seed beetle lives and mates on Gleditsia pods in the canopy without flower-visiting.
Phoretic mites live sub-cortically on spruce bark; tree-bark beats the default anywhere.
Lime hawk-moth caterpillar feeds motionlessly in Tilia foliage — not a flower-visitor.
Arboreal dormouse moves through canopy and occasionally forages on the ground.
Specialist aphid colonies are stationary on Carpinus leaf undersides.
Cherry blackfly forms dense stationary colonies on Prunus shoot tips and leaf undersides.
Pontania gallers develop inside static leaf galls on willow — no flower-visiting.
Gregarious sawfly larvae feed motionlessly on Robinia leaflets — not pollinators.
Scale insect is permanently fused to Magnolia bark — no movement whatsoever.

reason: Roosting bats anchor to tree cavities by day and fly aerial loops at night.
Noctule roosts in tree holes (idle-only) and hunts on high fast aerial circuits (sky-looper).
Adults glide between canopy trees and sip sap/nectar — larvae feed in foliage.
